content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity Project Template to Solve Deployments
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment bottlenecks. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to ship production-ready apps at lightning speed.
content.answer_block: The 'can't deploy' bottleneck usually stems from environment drift and misconfigured CI/CD pipelines. By adopting the Antigravity project template, you standardize your infrastructure-as-code and containerization. When paired with Claude Code, you automate the entire deployment lifecycle, ensuring your code moves from local development to production with zero friction.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, your client is waiting, and your deployment pipeline is throwing cryptic errors that defy logic. You've built a masterpiece, but it's trapped in your local environment, held hostage by configuration hell. This isn't just a technical glitch; it is a direct threat to your freelance reputation and your bottom line.

But what if you could bypass the chaos? The Antigravity project template isn't just a boilerplate; it is a battlefield-tested architecture designed to eliminate deployment friction. By leveraging modern orchestration and AI-assisted automation, you can transform your delivery process from a manual nightmare into a silent, automated background task.

steps.title: Initialize with Antigravity
steps.description: Standardize your project structure to ensure environment parity across dev, staging, and prod.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity standard template with support for Docker-based infrastructure and automated CI/CD workflows.

steps.title: Configure Infrastructure as Code
steps.description: Define your infrastructure requirements to ensure your deployment target is perfectly aligned with your code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a terraform/deployment manifest that aligns with the current repository structure and handles environment variable injection securely.

steps.title: Automate Deployment Hooks
steps.description: Integrate deployment triggers that fire upon successful branch merges to eliminate manual intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that detects changes in the Antigravity config and triggers a rolling deployment to the staging environment.

steps.title: Validate Environment Parity
steps.description: Ensure your local container environment exactly mirrors your production cloud environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current Dockerfile and environment configuration to ensure zero drift between local and production settings.

steps.title: Execute Production Push
steps.description: Run the final deployment sequence using Claude Code to monitor for post-deployment health checks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Execute the production deployment sequence and monitor the health check endpoints for 200 OK responses.

pro_tips: Always use environment-specific secret stores rather than .env files to prevent credential leakage in your repo.
Implement a 'dry-run' deployment script to test infrastructure changes against the cloud provider's API before committing code.
Use Claude Code to write custom health-check scripts that ping your API endpoints immediately after a deployment completes.
Leverage Antigravity's built-in caching layers to reduce build times by up to 70% during iterative client updates.
Tag your production deployments with git-hash metadata so you can roll back to a known-good state in under 30 seconds.
content.next_steps: After completing your first successful deployment, document the process in a README.md and share it with your clients as a 'Professional Delivery Protocol' to increase your perceived value.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting infrastructure hold your code hostage. Master the deployment stack and scale your freelance business with elite tooling. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
